topic: Sharp identifiability of an interventional causal effect from a heterogeneous collection of k partially-overlapping observational distributions under a selection-diagram constraint set: a closed-form iff identifiability characterization extending Bareinboim-Pearl (2014, 2016) data fusion from two sources to k sources, with a polynomial-time decision algorithm in the size of the diagram and an explicit counterexample witness construction certifying non-identifiability when the iff fails, recovering Bareinboim-Pearl as the k=2 special case

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize sharp bounds for a long-term interventional effect when short-run surrogate panels, bridge or validation laws, and weakly aligned data-fusion sources are jointly available but do not point-identify the target.

source_refs.claim: Li and Luedtke characterize efficiency gains from fusing multiple aligned data sources for smooth parameters, leaving sharp identified sets under bridge failure or bounded drift outside the point-ID efficiency setup.
source_refs.author: Sijia Li and Alex Luedtke

source_refs.excerpt: sharp lower and upper bounds on the long-term ATE are exactly the optimum values of two linear programs
LiLuedtke2023 study efficient data fusion with surrogate information and limited outcome data

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published efficiency result assumes source alignment conditions strong enough to deliver a regular point estimand, while the prior CausalSmith angle explicitly shifts to finite bridge, measurement, and bounded-drift restrictions where latent long-run outcomes are only partially pinned down. The unresolved obstacle is not estimation but sharpness: the same surrogate and validation summaries must be shown to describe exactly the feasible latent-completion polytope, including all cross-source compatibility restrictions.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A closed-form or finite-LP sharp identified set for the long-term ATE, with an attainability proof for every bridge, measurement, and drift-compatible latent law.

open_problems.open_problem: Find an explicit binary transport endpoint formula and counterexample witnesses for strict panel-surrogate tightening over terminal-only copula bounds.

source_refs.excerpt: The full-panel interval is strictly shorter if and only if the drift budget satisfies
Add a binary numerical boundary example showing one terminal-only endpoint face removed

open_problems.why_unsolved: The reviewer-prescribed strengthening asks for an observable boundary example because the rejected angle had a plausible transport formula but lacked a witness separating full-panel surrogate information from terminal-only endpoint information. The concrete obstacle is a coupling constraint: observed increments across the surrogate panel must remove a terminal copula face without assuming the desired endpoint formula.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Exact 2-by-2 coupling constraints plus two observed-increment witness laws certifying when terminal-only endpoints cannot attain the full-panel bound.

open_problems.open_problem: Give a closed-form necessary-and-sufficient characterization for identifying the target interventional law from k partially overlapping observational source distributions governed by a selection-diagram constraint set.

source_refs.claim: Bareinboim and Pearl frame data fusion as combining heterogeneous populations, regimes, and sampling processes, but the paper is not a k-source observational iff criterion with witness construction.
source_refs.author: Elias Bareinboim and Judea Pearl

source_refs.claim: Bareinboim and Pearl prove completeness for transportability from multiple environments with limited experiments, a different regime from purely observational partially overlapping source laws.
source_refs.author: Elias Bareinboim and Judea Pearl

source_refs.claim: Tikka, Hyttinen, and Karvanen give a sound do-calculus search over arbitrary observational and experimental source distributions, with completeness only for classes already characterized by do-calculus.
source_refs.author: Santtu Tikka, Antti Hyttinen, and Juha Karvanen

open_problems.why_unsolved: The closest complete graphical results either use experimental source information or solve transportability and selection-bias fragments separately; the closest arbitrary-input tool searches symbolic do-calculus derivations rather than giving a closed-form k-source criterion. The missing assumption class is a clean observational-only selection-diagram model in which each source reveals only a subset or selected law, yet the algorithm returns a formula exactly when all compatible SCMs agree on the target effect.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A graph-theoretic iff criterion with a constructive identifying functional over the available source marginals and conditionals, specializing to the Bareinboim-Pearl two-source case.

open_problems.open_problem: Construct a canonical non-identifiability certificate for k-source observational data fusion under selection diagrams.

source_refs.claim: Shpitser and Pearl give hedge-style completeness witnesses for ordinary interventional identification, but not for multiple overlapping selected source laws.
source_refs.author: Ilya Shpitser and Judea Pearl

source_refs.claim: Bareinboim and Pearl establish completeness for transportability, but the obstruction is not stated as a k-source observational witness preserving all partially overlapping source distributions.
source_refs.author: Elias Bareinboim and Judea Pearl

open_problems.why_unsolved: Existing hedge and transportability obstructions certify failures relative to one observational law plus a specified collection of experimental or source-domain quantities. In k-source observational fusion, a failure witness must keep every observed source margin or selected conditional law fixed while changing the target intervention, so the ordinary hedge must be refined to encode source coverage, selection nodes, and cross-source equality constraints.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A generalized multisource hedge that produces two finite SCMs compatible with the same k source distributions and selection constraints but disagreeing on the target interventional law.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize identification when k observational source distributions differ simultaneously by transportability mechanisms and by source-specific selection mechanisms.

source_refs.claim: Bareinboim, Tian, and Pearl give complete graphical and algorithmic conditions for recovering conditional probabilities from selection-biased data, plus partial-source unbiased-data conditions.
source_refs.author: Elias Bareinboim, Jin Tian, and Judea Pearl

source_refs.claim: Bareinboim and Tian completely characterize recoverable effects in Markovian models with simultaneous confounding and selection, and give sufficient conditions for semi-Markovian models.
source_refs.author: Elias Bareinboim and Jin Tian

source_refs.claim: Tikka, Hyttinen, and Karvanen explicitly list combined transportability and selection bias and multiple sources of selection bias as challenging extensions not previously considered in the literature.
source_refs.author: Santtu Tikka, Antti Hyttinen, and Juha Karvanen

open_problems.why_unsolved: Selection recoverability and transportability have mature isolated theories, but their intersection with multiple observational sources leaves two mechanisms active at once: source domains may differ structurally from the target, and each source may also be selected on different variables. The obstacle is deciding which discrepancies can be neutralized by cross-source overlap and which induce a genuine target-effect ambiguity without relying on exhaustive symbolic search.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A polynomial-time decision algorithm returning either a source-fusion identifying formula or a multisource selection/transport obstruction.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive sharp bounds for the target interventional effect when k-source selection-diagram point identification fails.

source_refs.claim: Lanners, Rudin, Volfovsky, and Parikh propose partial-identification data fusion when no-unobserved-confounding and cross-source exchangeability can fail together, but not arbitrary selection-diagram k-source bounds.
source_refs.author: Quinn Lanners, Cynthia Rudin, Alexander Volfovsky, and Harsh Parikh

source_refs.claim: Zetterstrom, Sjolander, and Waernbaum study improved and sometimes sharp bounds under selection bias with sensitivity parameters, not arbitrary multi-source selected observational laws.
source_refs.author: Stina Zetterstrom, Arvid Sjolander, and Ingeborg Waernbaum

source_refs.claim: Manski's partial-identification program supplies the sharp-set viewpoint, but not graph-specific k-source selection-diagram optimization.
source_refs.author: Charles F. Manski

open_problems.why_unsolved: Graphical identification algorithms usually stop at formula-or-failure, while recent data-fusion bounds impose exchangeability or sensitivity structures tailored to trial/observational combinations. The unresolved obstacle is optimizing over all SCMs that simultaneously match several overlapping selected source laws and latent-confounding constraints, then proving attainability rather than only outer validity.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp identified set for the target interventional law, expressed as analytic bounds or a finite-dimensional optimization with a proof that every endpoint is attained by a compatible SCM.

open_problems.open_problem: State the minimal positivity and support-overlap regimes under which k-source graphical fusion formulas are valid.

source_refs.claim: Crump, Hotz, Imbens, and Mitnik address limited overlap by changing the estimand, highlighting support as a first-order obstacle outside purely graphical criteria.
source_refs.author: Richard K. Crump, V. Joseph Hotz, Guido W. Imbens, and Oscar A. Mitnik

source_refs.claim: Degtiar and Rose review generalizability and transportability methods where overlap and target-population support conditions are central assumptions.
source_refs.author: Irina Degtiar and Sherri Rose
source_refs.venue: Annual Review of Statistics and Its Application

source_refs.claim: Colnet et al. review methods combining randomized trials and observational studies, emphasizing practical data-combination assumptions rather than arbitrary selection-diagram completeness.
source_refs.author: Benedicte Colnet, Imke Mayer, Guanhua Chen, Awa Dieng, Ruohong Li, Gael Varoquaux, Jean-Philippe Vert, Julie Josse, and Shu Yang

open_problems.why_unsolved: Graphical identifiability can certify algebraic recoverability while hiding denominators or conditional laws that no source observes on the needed support. In partially overlapping observational collections, a formula may require ratios across source-specific supports or selected subpopulations, so the same graph criterion needs a companion support theorem distinguishing point identification, estimand change, and partial identification.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An identification formula annotated with minimal source-specific positivity conditions and a regime statement showing when support failure converts point identification into non-identification or partial identification.

literature_map:
Bareinboim and Pearl (2016, PNAS) frames data fusion as combining heterogeneous populations, regimes, and sampling processes.
Pearl and Bareinboim (2014, Statistical Science) and Bareinboim and Pearl (2012, AAAI) establish selection-diagram transportability and complete algorithms for source-target transport.
Bareinboim and Pearl (2014, NeurIPS) gives completeness for mz-transportability, but the setting is built around experimental or limited experimental information, not purely observational k-source partial overlap.
Lee and Honavar (2013, AAAI) studies m-transportability from multiple environments and supplies another multi-environment anchor.
Bareinboim, Tian, and Pearl (2014, AAAI) and Bareinboim and Tian (2015, AAAI) handle selection-bias recoverability, but not arbitrary k-source observational fusion.
Correa, Tian, and Bareinboim (2018, AAAI) gives complete generalized adjustment under selection and confounding, a narrower adjustment fragment.
Shpitser and Pearl (2006, AAAI; 2008, JMLR) and Huang and Valtorta (2006, AAAI) provide ordinary-ID completeness and hedge/counterexample substrate.
Tikka, Hyttinen, and Karvanen (2021, JSS) is the closest arbitrary-input tool: do-search handles multiple incomplete observational and experimental distributions, but it is not the desired closed-form polynomial iff characterization.
Li and Luedtke (2023, Biometrika) and Li et al. (2023, arXiv) show modern statistical data-fusion efficiency with aligned or weakly aligned sources, distinct from sharp nonparametric bounds under bridge/drift failure.
Econometric external-validity anchors include Hotz, Imbens, and Mortimer (2005), Heckman and Vytlacil (2005), and Bisbee, Dehejia, Pop-Eleches, and Samii (2017), emphasizing heterogeneity, support, and extrapolation rather than graphical iff criteria.
Recent reviews by Degtiar and Rose (2023) and Colnet et al. (2024) show applied growth in transportability and trial/observational combination, mostly with exchangeability or weighting assumptions.
Partial-identification work from Manski to Lanners et al. (2025) and Zetterstrom et al. (2025) supplies a fallback when point identification fails, but sharp bounds for arbitrary k selected overlapping sources remain open.
prior_proposal_map:
Inspected active research/eid_data_fusion_k_source: only state/pipeline metadata, no proposal content yet.
Searched banked active/failed/downgraded proposals, rejected angle tex files, reviews, gaps JSON, READMEs, and pipeline JSONLs under doc/formalization.
No direct prior proposal on Bareinboim-Pearl k-source selection diagrams was found.
Highest-yield direct data-fusion hit: pid_long_term_surrogate, with bridge-law/surrogate-panel fusion, transport endpoints, and reviewer-requested witnesses.
Closest exact-ID graph/proxy hits: eid_proximal_phase and eid_frontdoor_multi_mediator, both about nonunique bridges/proxy sharpness rather than selection diagrams.
Closest exact/partial identification boundary hits: eid_dist_synth_control, eid_shift_share_design, and eid_continuous_did_acr_panel.
Adjacent reusable sharpness gaps: dynamic-IV joint projection, continuous-dose CMSM pasting, proximal mediation zonotopes, and QTE finite-Gamma endpoint boundaries.
